definition:
1. one who stands before, a front-rank-man , (Xenophon Historicus) 
2. a chief, leader  of a party, (Herdotus Historicus); ὁ πρ. τοῦ δήμου (Thucydides) 
3. generally, a president, ruler , (Aeschulus Tragicus), etc.; προστάται τῆς εἰρήνης its chief authors , (Xenophon Historicus) 
4. one who stands before, a protector, guard, champion , πυλωμάτων (Aeschulus Tragicus), etc. 
5. at Athens, of a citizen who took care of the  μέτοικοι, as the Rom. patronus  took care of his clientes;  προστάτην γράφεσθαί τινα to choose as one's patron , (Aristophanes Comicus); but, γράφεσθαι προστάτου to enter oneself by one's patron's name , attach oneself to a patron , (Sophocles Tragicus) 
6. προστάτης θεοῦ one who stands before  a god to entreat him, a suppliant , (Sophocles Tragicus) (ML)
